steps.title: Find the line from the two table points
steps.content: The two visible points are $\left(-11,27+\frac{11}{27}\right)$ and $\left(-1,27+\frac{1}{27}\right)$. The slope is $$\frac{\left(27+\frac{1}{27}\right)-\left(27+\frac{11}{27}\right)}{-1-(-11)}=\frac{-\frac{10}{27}}{10}=-\frac{1}{27}.$$ Since the line is at $27$ when $x=0$, the equation is $y=-\frac{x}{27}+27$.

steps.title: Find the x-intercept
steps.content: The x-intercept is where $y=0$. From $y=-\frac{x}{27}+27$, set $0=-\frac{x}{27}+27$. Then $\frac{x}{27}=27$, so $x=729$. The intercept is $(729,0)$.
